text.title: Persona Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: Two worked persona examples with evidence-anchored structure, and antipatterns that produce fictional or useless personas.

example.note: Persona: Newsletter Nate (SaaS for freelance writers). Based on 14 interviews.
example.code:
## Newsletter Nate
**Who:** 34-year-old freelance writer running a paid newsletter with 2,000 subscribers ($4K/month from writing).
**Goals:** Grow subscriber base to 10,000; reduce time on operations; launch a course.
**Frustrations:** Email platform is expensive at scale (INT-003, INT-007, INT-011); no easy way to sell digital products (INT-002, INT-009); analytics are confusing (INT-005).
**Quote (INT-007):** "I'm a writer, not a tech person. I just want it to work."
**Buying trigger:** Switches when current tool fails or pricing surprise hits at a growth milestone.
**Falsification:** If >30% of new signups are NOT running a paid newsletter, this persona is wrong.

example.note: Persona: Agency Amy (project management for agencies). Based on 11 interviews.
example.code:
## Agency Amy
**Who:** 38-year-old agency owner, 5-person design agency, $500K annual revenue.
**Goals:** Stop projects going over budget (INT-001, INT-006); improve client communication (INT-004); scale without adding overhead (INT-008, INT-010).
**Frustrations:** Team uses 6 different tools (INT-001, INT-003, INT-008); clients don't understand project status (INT-004, INT-006).
**Quote (INT-003):** "I need my team aligned without micromanaging."
**Buying trigger:** Current tool failure causes a client escalation or budget overrun.

antipattern.description: Persona with stock photo, alliterative name, and invented demographics not drawn from interviews.
antipattern.reason: Polished presentation signals quality but predicts nothing; invented detail justifies wrong decisions with false confidence.

antipattern.description: Creating 5 or more personas before reaching $10K MRR or equivalent traction.
antipattern.reason: Multiple personas at pre-traction stage fragment focus; start with one primary and expand only after traction confirms segment boundaries.
Personas frozen at founding data echo year-one assumptions in year-three decisions; require quarterly minor refresh and post-pivot major rebuild.
Without defining who not to target, teams waste acquisition budget on high-churn, high-support-cost, price-sensitive users that fit no sustainable segment.

see-also.link: Anthropic SDK script for transcript clustering
